meta_title: For Students: deployment workflow to Solve can't debug code (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to deployment workflow and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Deployment Workflow: Fix Debugging Issues (Student Guide)
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with bugs. Learn a professional deployment workflow to debug your code efficiently using Antigravity and Claude Code. Perfect for students.
content.answer_block: Debugging is not about staring at code; it is about observability. By integrating a structured deployment workflow, you isolate failures in staging environments. Use Claude Code to automate bug detection and Antigravity to simulate production traffic, ensuring your local logic translates perfectly to the live user environment every time.
content.intro:
You are staring at a screen where the code works perfectly on your machine, but the moment you push to production, it collapses into a silent void of errors. The frustration of 'it works on my machine' is the rite of passage for every student, but it is also the primary barrier to shipping your first real-world SaaS application.

True engineering isn't just about writing features; it is about building a feedback loop that makes errors visible before they reach your users. By mastering a deployment workflow that forces your code through rigorous validation, you transform from a student guessing at syntax into an AI builder who owns the entire lifecycle of their software.

steps.title: Establish Environment Parity
steps.description: Ensure your local development environment mirrors production to prevent configuration drift that causes elusive bugs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current project structure and generate a Dockerfile and docker-compose.yml that standardizes my local dev environment to match production Node.js/Python specs.

steps.title: Automated Linting and Type Checking
steps.description: Catch syntax and logic errors before deployment by enforcing strict static analysis in your CI/CD pipeline.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a pre-commit hook configuration that runs ESLint and TypeScript compilation to prevent any code with type errors from being committed.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'npm run lint && npm run build'

steps.title: Implement Observability Logging
steps.description: Inject structured logging into your application so you can trace the execution flow when a production error occurs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor my current error handling to use Winston or PINO structured logging, ensuring all critical function entry and exit points are traced.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Add structured logging to the auth controller'

steps.title: Simulate Production Traffic
steps.description: Use load testing tools to see how your application handles stress, which often triggers race conditions or memory leaks.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a load testing script using k6 to simulate 50 concurrent users accessing my primary API endpoint to check for stability.

steps.title: Automated Rollback Verification
steps.description: Define a strategy to revert deployments instantly if your automated smoke tests fail post-deployment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a GitHub Actions workflow that executes a smoke test after deployment and triggers an automatic rollback if the health check endpoint returns a 500.

pro_tips: Always use a .env.example file to track required variables; hardcoded secrets are the silent killers of production deployments.
Implement 'feature flags' to toggle new code paths without needing to redeploy the entire application stack.
Treat your deployment logs as your primary debugging tool; if it isn't logged, it didn't happen.
Use 'dry-run' flags on all your CLI deployment tools to preview changes before they impact your live database.
Adopt the '12-factor app' methodology to ensure your service is inherently scalable and easy to debug.
content.next_steps: Once you have verified your deployment pipeline, focus on setting up a staging environment that mirrors your production database with sanitized mock data to test edge cases.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting your code and start commanding your deployment. You have the tools, the workflow, and the vision to launch your first app. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
